key_insight: A multi-LLM pipeline can generate and quality-check paired 3D brain MRI reports, enabling visual instruction tuning for oncology-focused vision-language models.
clinical_relevance: This approach may improve MRI report generation and visual question answering for glioma and meningioma, supporting more consistent imaging interpretation in brain oncology.
method: Built a new 3D MRI-text dataset from glioma and meningioma cases.
Used multiple LLMs cooperatively to draft, verify, and refine reports.
Trained a VLM to tokenize MRI scans and align them with text instructions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the proposed VLM outperformed other 2D and 3D methods on report generation and visual question answering tasks.
watch_item: The approach depends on paired 3D MRI-text data and cooperative LLM report generation, so data quality and generalizability across sites and scanners remain important concerns.
paper_title: Multi-LLM Collaborative MRI Report Generation for Visual Instruction Tuning in Brain Oncology
